terminology.definition: Interface layer supporting multiple AI model providers (OpenClaw, Hermes, Claude Code, Codex) with unified lifecycle operations.
Complete operational cycle from installation through execution to cleanup, including install, start, stop, and uninstall phases.
Creation and configuration of specialized AI agents with defined roles, workspaces, and tool permissions within workflows.
Atomic task execution unit with retry logic, template resolution, output parsing, and failure handling within workflow steps.
Orchestrates agent execution through child processes with concurrency control, output streaming, and lifecycle management.
Time-based automation system for agent heartbeats, polling, and continuous task execution using scheduled cron jobs.
Declarative definition of complete workflows including agents, steps, roles, and execution constraints.
Context-aware substitution engine for placeholder variables and template expansion in step inputs and configurations.
Safety constraints limiting agent capabilities based on designated roles (analysis, coding, verification, testing).
BDD-style story execution with lifecycle tracking, acceptance criteria validation, and status management.
